title: 11 Best Herbal Teas For Asthma
intro: Herbal teas for Asthma are a type of natural remedy that has been used to alleviate symptoms of the respiratory condition, characterized by episodes of wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath. These teas can effectively treat asthma due to their anti-inflammatory, bronchodilatory, and expectorant properties, which help to relax the airways, reduce inflammation, and promote mucus clearance. Examples of herbal teas that can be used to treat asthma include Peppermint tea, which relaxes the airways and eases coughing, Ginger tea, which reduces inflammation and congestion, Eucalyptus tea, which opens up the airways and relieves congestion, Thyme tea, which has antibacterial properties that help combat infections, and Licorice root tea, which reduces inflammation and promotes expectoration. Additionally, other herbal teas such as Sage, Oregano, and Echinacea are also commonly used to treat asthma due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used to treat asthma due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving symptoms of asthma. The bioactive constituents of Eucalyptus globulus, including eucalyptol, limonene, and cineole, have been shown to help relax bronchial muscles, reduce mucus production, and improve respiratory function. Regular consumption of Eucalyptus globulus teas can provide relief from asthma symptoms, improve lung function, and reduce the need for medication, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves.
Measure 1 teaspoon of Eucalyptus globulus leaves per cup of boiling water.
Heat 1 cup of water in a kettle or pot.
Steep the Eucalyptus globulus leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day, as needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "African journal of traditional, complementary, and alternative medicines : AJTCAM", Eucalyptus globulus teas was identified as one of the most important species used by Togolese traditional healers to treat asthma, based on its calculated use values.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can be effective in treating asthma symptoms, but it may cause side effects such as nausea, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or for prolonged periods. To minimize potential risks, it is essential to start with a low dose, monitor your body's response, and avoid consuming the tea during pregnancy and breastfeeding, as well as with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Eucalyptus globulus tea for asthma treatment.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried leaves in 1 cup of boiling water daily.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, 1 hour after meals, for 1-2 weeks.
Monitor asthma symptoms and adjust the dosage to 1-2 teaspoons per cup as needed.
Combine Eucalyptus globulus tea with other asthma treatments under medical supervision.

preparation_amazon.title: Jovvily Eucalyptus Leaves - 1lb - Dried - Cut & Sifted - Herbal Tea

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, also known as thyme tea, have been traditionally used to treat asthma due to their bronchodilatory, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat asthma by relaxing the airway muscles, reducing inflammation and congestion, and combating infections that can exacerbate the condition. The bioactive constituents of thyme tea, including thymol, carvacrol, and rosmarinic acid, contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting the release of histamine and other inflammatory mediators, as well as by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The benefits of using thymus vulgaris teas to treat asthma include improved lung function, reduced symptoms and frequency of asthma attacks, and a decrease in the need for conventional medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus vulgaris leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Add 1-2 tablespoons of fresh leaves or 1/2 teaspoon of dried leaves to the boiling water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea if using fresh leaves.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage asthma symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to The Journal of asthma : official journal of the Association for the Care of Asthma, Thymus vulgaris teas for asthma have been shown to have positive effects on bronchodilation, pulmonary function tests, and antagonism of asthma mediators such as histamine and platelet activating factor.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can be used to treat asthma, but it may cause side effects such as dry mouth, nausea, and increased heart rate, as well as allergic reactions like hives and itching in some individuals. When using Thymus vulgaris teas to treat asthma, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase it to avoid over-stimulation of the respiratory system, and to avoid consumption if you have high blood pressure or are taking medications that may interact with the herbal preparation.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Thymus vulgaris tea for asthma treatment.
Drink 1 cup of Thymus vulgaris tea 2-3 times a day to help ease symptoms.
Start with a small dosage of 1 teaspoon of dried leaves per cup of hot water.
Gradually increase the dosage to 2-3 teaspoons per cup as needed and under doctor's guidance.
Monitor your asthma symptoms and adjust your dosage and frequency under doctor's supervision.

preparation_amazon.title: Buddha Teas Organic Thyme Leaf Tea - OU Kosher, USDA Organic, CCOF Organic, 18 Bleach-Free Tea Bags

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of asthma due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents, including alkylamides, caffeic acid, and rosmarinic acid, play a crucial role in reducing inflammation and modulating the immune system, thereby helping to alleviate asthma symptoms. By reducing inflammation in the airways and modulating the immune system, Echinacea purpurea teas may help to prevent asthma attacks and improve overall respiratory function. Regular consumption of Echinacea purpurea teas may provide relief from asthma symptoms and improve quality of life for individuals suffering from this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea root and flowers.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the Echinacea purpurea to the boiling water and reduce heat.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed for asthma relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the provided study, Echinacea purpurea teas have potential as an adjuvant symptomatic therapy in asthma, suggesting a possible use in its treatment.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to help alleviate asthma symptoms, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, stomach upset, and headaches due to its potent compounds. Precautions to take when using Echinacea purpurea teas for asthma treatment include being cautious when brewing it to avoid over-steeping, which can lead to excessive levels of its active compounds.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Echinacea purpurea teas for asthma.
Take 1-2 teaspoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes and strain the tea.
Drink 1-2 cups of Echinacea tea, 2-3 times a day, as needed.
Monitor your symptoms and adjust dosage with your doctor's guidance.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, derived from the leaves of the rosemary plant, have been traditionally used to treat asthma and other respiratory issues. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviate asthma symptoms. The bioactive constituents, including carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, possess anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidative properties that help to calm the airways and reduce the frequency and severity of asthma attacks. Regular consumption of Rosmarinus officinalis teas has been shown to provide relief from asthma symptoms, improve lung function, and enhance overall respiratory health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves.
Steep 1 tablespoon of Rosmarinus officinalis leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed to help manage asthma symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be beneficial in treating asthma, but it may cause side effects such as increased heart rate, high blood pressure, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions include using it in moderation, avoiding it during pregnancy and breastfeeding, and being aware of potential interactions with other medications, such as blood thinners, and certain asthma medications.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Rosmarinus officinalis teas for asthma treatment.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried leaves per cup of boiling water, steep for 5-7 minutes.
Drink 1-2 cups of tea 2-3 times a day, as recommended by doctor.
Monitor symptoms and adjust dosage as needed, under doctor's guidance.
Continue treatment for 2-4 weeks, and then reassess symptoms with doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat asthma due to their anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which help to relax the airways and improve breathing. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that contribute to their therapeutic effects on asthma. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation in the airway muscles, Lavandula angustifolia teas help to alleviate symptoms of asthma, such as wheezing and shortness of breath. Regular consumption of these teas may also help to reduce the frequency and severity of asthma attacks, providing relief to individuals suffering from this chronic condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers and a tea infuser.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers to the tea infuser.
Steep the flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1 cup as needed, up to 3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can potentially be used to alleviate asthma symptoms, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat asthma, including avoiding consumption if you have a history of liver or kidney problems, and being cautious when driving or operating heavy machinery due to its potential sedative effects.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia tea, 2-3 times a day.
Start with a low dose of 1/4 teaspoon of dried flowers per cup.
Gradually increase dose to 1/2 teaspoon per cup if needed.
Consume tea 30 minutes before meals for better absorption.
Consult doctor before making Lavandula angustifolia tea a regular asthma treatment.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger teas, have been traditionally used to treat asthma due to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and bronchodilatory properties. The bioactive constituents of ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to relax the airway muscles and reduce inflammation, thereby making breathing easier for individuals with asthma. These constituents also possess antimicrobial properties that help to prevent infections that can exacerbate asthma symptoms. Drinking ginger tea regularly has been shown to provide significant relief from asthma symptoms, making it a beneficial herbal preparation for managing this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root powder and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the powder in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the mixture with a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, to make the tea more palatable.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally before meals, to help manage asthma symptoms.
Consult with a healthcare professional or a qualified herbalist before making Zingiber officinale tea a regular part of your treatment plan.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Recent patents on biotechnology", Zingiber officinale teas for asthma may offer potential as an adjuvant symptomatic therapy, given its bioactive compounds and therapeutic applications in treating respiratory conditions.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat asthma, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high ginger content. When using Zingiber officinale teas to treat asthma, precautions should be taken to avoid consuming it in large quantities, especially on an empty stomach, to prevent digestive issues and allergic reactions.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Zingiber officinale tea for asthma treatment.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried ginger root in 1 cup of boiling water.
Drink 1-2 cups of tea, 2-3 times a day, as needed.
Increase ginger root to 2 teaspoons if symptoms persist after 3 days.
Monitor asthma symptoms and adjust dosage or consult a doctor if necessary.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been traditionally used to treat asthma, a chronic respiratory condition characterized by inflammation and airway constriction. The anti-inflammatory properties of glycyrrhizin, a bioactive constituent found in this herbal preparation, help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. The spasmolytic and bronchodilatory effects of glycyrrhizin and other bioactive constituents, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, also help to relax the airway muscles and improve airflow, providing relief from asthma symptoms. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat asthma include reduced symptoms, improved lung function, and a decrease in the frequency and severity of asthma attacks.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser.
Place the dried root in the tea infuser.
Steep the root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the root.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve asthma symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Current pharmaceutical design", Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for asthma have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidative properties that can counterbalance the inflammation, oxidative stress, allergic response, tracheal smooth muscle cell constriction and airway remodeling associated with asthma.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat asthma, but they may cause side effects such as increased blood pressure, potassium deficiency, and changes in hormone levels, particularly when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To use Glycyrrhiza glabra teas safely, it is recommended to start with low doses and gradually increase as needed, and to be aware of the potential interactions with other medications and to drink plenty of water to minimize the risk of dehydration.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Glycyrrhiza glabra tea for asthma
Drink 1/2 cup of brewed tea 2-3 times a day
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried roots per cup of boiling water
Maintain dosage for 2-4 weeks for best results
Monitor symptoms and adjust dosage under doctor's guidance

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas, derived from the bark of the Cinchona tree, have been used for centuries to treat asthma due to their anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties. The bioactive constituents, such as quinine, quinidine, and caffeine, help to relax the airway muscles, improve breathing, and reduce inflammation in the lungs, making it easier to manage asthma symptoms. The benefits of Cinchona officinalis teas in treating asthma include reduced frequency and severity of asthma attacks, improved lung function, and enhanced overall respiratory health. By incorporating Cinchona officinalis teas into a treatment plan, individuals with asthma may experience improved quality of life and reduced reliance on conventional medications.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried Cinchona officinalis bark. You can buy it from a health store or online.
Boil the water in a pot. Add the dried Cinchona officinalis bark and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a tea strainer or cheesecloth into a cup. Discard the bark.
Add honey or sugar to taste, if needed. Stir well to dissolve.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, to help manage asthma symptoms. Consult a doctor before using it as a treatment.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "The Journal of asthma : official journal of the Association for the Care of Asthma", Cinchona officinalis teas for asthma showed benefit as a primary or adjunctive treatment in studies reviewed, but study quality and safety information were mixed.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be used to treat asthma, but possible side effects may include dizziness, headaches, and nausea due to the high quinine content. Precautions to take when using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat asthma include avoiding excessive consumption, which can lead to interactions with other medications and worsen underlying conditions, and being cautious when consuming it during pregnancy or breastfeeding.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Cinchona officinalis tea daily for asthma relief.
Start with 1 teaspoon of Cinchona bark per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes and strain the tea before drinking.
Take tea 2-3 times a day, or as directed by a healthcare professional.
Monitor your symptoms and adjust the dosage with guidance from a healthcare expert.

preparations.herb_desc: Sambucus nigra teas have been traditionally used to help alleviate asthma symptoms due to their anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which help to relax airway muscles and improve breathing. The bioactive constituents of Sambucus nigra, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, work synergistically to modulate the immune system and reduce inflammation in the airways. The anthocyanins present in Sambucus nigra teas have been shown to have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to protect the lungs from oxidative stress and inflammation associated with asthma. By consuming Sambucus nigra teas, individuals with asthma may experience reduced symptoms, improved lung function, and a decrease in the frequency and severity of asthma attacks.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Sambucus nigra flowers. You can buy them at a health food store or online.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried flowers and put them in a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth.
Boil 1 cup of water and pour it over the Sambucus nigra flowers in the tea infuser or cheesecloth.
Let the tea steep for 5-7 minutes. Then, strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help with asthma symptoms. You can add honey to taste.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Sambucus nigra teas can help alleviate asthma symptoms, but it may cause side effects such as drowsiness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals due to its antihistamine and antispasmodic properties. Precautions to take when using Sambucus nigra teas to treat asthma include monitoring blood pressure and heart rate, as it may interact with medications and exacerbate conditions like high blood pressure, and avoiding consumption in pregnant or breastfeeding women.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Sambucus nigra tea for asthma.
Start with 1 cup of tea per day, taken 30 minutes before meals.
Gradually increase to 2 cups per day, if symptoms improve.
Drink tea for 3-6 months, as directed by doctor or manufacturer.
Monitor symptoms and adjust dosage under doctor's guidance.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been used for centuries to treat various health conditions, including asthma, due to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and bronchodilatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba, such as flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide, help to relax airway muscles, reduce inflammation, and increase the production of nitric oxide, making it easier for air to flow into the lungs. By consuming Ginkgo biloba teas, individuals with asthma may experience relief from symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath, as well as improved lung function and overall quality of life. The benefits of using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat asthma include reduced reliance on medication, improved breathing, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Steep 2 tablespoons of the leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Add honey to taste, if desired, to sweeten the tea.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage asthma symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Zhongguo Zhong xi yi jie he za zhi Zhongguo Zhongxiyi jiehe zazhi = Chinese journal of integrated traditional and Western medicine", Ginkgo biloba teas for asthma significantly reduced airway hyperreactivity, improved clinical symptoms, and pulmonary functions of asthmatic patients.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can potentially exacerbate asthma symptoms and interact with medications used to treat the condition, such as theophylline and beta-agonists, leading to increased heart rate, palpitations, and tremors. Precautions include monitoring blood pressure and heart rate closely, and avoiding Ginkgo biloba teas if you have a history of cardiovascular disease, as it may increase the risk of bleeding and interact with blood-thinning medications.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Ginkgo biloba tea for asthma treatment.
Start with 1 teaspoon (5 ml) of tea, 2-3 times a day, in 1 cup of water.
Monitor symptoms and adjust dosage to 1-2 tablespoons (15-30 ml) if needed.
Use tea for 2-4 weeks, then reassess symptoms with doctor's guidance.
Combine Ginkgo biloba tea with conventional asthma treatment under doctor's supervision.

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm, have been traditionally used to treat asthma due to their anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Melissa officinalis, including rosmarinic acid, apigenin, and luteolin, help to relax bronchial muscles, reduce inflammation, and improve airflow in the lungs. By consuming Melissa officinalis teas, individuals with asthma may experience relief from symptoms such as wheezing and shortness of breath, and may also benefit from improved sleep quality and reduced stress levels. Overall, Melissa officinalis teas offer a natural and potentially effective way to manage asthma symptoms, making them a popular herbal remedy among those seeking alternative treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers. You can use fresh or dried leaves.
Use a tablespoon to measure 2-3 tablespoons of Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot. Do not boil.
Add the measured Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers to the water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it while it's warm. You can drink it 2-3 times a day to help with asthma symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating asthma, but using it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Melissa officinalis teas safely, it is essential to note that excessive consumption can lead to an over-relaxation effect, interact with other medications, and worsen symptoms in people with liver or kidney problems, so it is recommended to consume it in moderation and avoid taking it with other sedative herbs or medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Melissa officinalis tea 2-3 times a day.
Take 1-2 teaspoons of dried Melissa officinalis flowers per cup.
Use as needed to relieve asthma symptoms and inflammation.
For severe asthma, drink 2-3 cups of tea per day.
Consult a healthcare professional for personalized usage and guidance.

preparations.herb_desc: Piper nigrum teas, derived from the black pepper plant, have been used for centuries to help alleviate asthma symptoms. The anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties of this herbal preparation help to treat asthma by relaxing airway muscles and reducing inflammation, making it easier to breathe. The bioactive constituents, including piperine and flavonoids, play a crucial role in exhibiting these beneficial effects. The benefits of Piper nigrum teas in treating asthma include improved lung function, reduced wheezing and coughing, and enhanced overall respiratory health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 1 tablespoon of black peppercorns (Piper nigrum), and a tea infuser or strainer.
Boil the water in a pot or kettle, then reduce heat to low.
Add the black peppercorns to the tea infuser or strainer, then place it into the pot.
Steep the black peppercorns for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser or strainer.
Strain the tea into a cup and drink immediately. Repeat as needed.

To use Piper nigrum teas safely, it is essential to start with small doses, gradually increase the amount, and avoid consuming it before bedtime or on an empty stomach to minimize potential discomfort.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as milk thistle teas, have been traditionally used to treat asthma due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents present in this herbal preparation, such as flavonoids and silymarin, help to reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate asthma symptoms. Silymarin, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the release of histamine and other chemical mediators that contribute to asthma exacerbations, thereby providing relief from symptoms such as wheezing and shortness of breath. The benefits of using Silybum marianum teas to treat asthma include reduced inflammation, improved lung function, and a decrease in the need for medication, making it a promising alternative treatment for this chronic condition.

Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it starts boiling.
Add the Silybum marianum leaves to the boiling water, then reduce heat to a simmer.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into a cup.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, to help manage asthma symptoms.

The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by relaxing the airway muscles, reducing inflammation, and improving lung function. The bioactive constituents, including menthol, menthone, and limonene, in Mentha x piperita teas contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting the release of histamine and other chemical mediators that exacerbate asthma symptoms. By incorporating Mentha x piperita teas into a treatment plan, patients may experience benefits such as reduced wheezing, improved breathing, and enhanced overall respiratory health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried peppermint leaves (Mentha x piperita).

Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into a cup.
Add honey to taste, if desired, to sweeten the tea.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage asthma symptoms.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Mentha x piperita teas can be used to alleviate asthma symptoms due to its anti-inflammatory properties, but consuming it excessively may lead to side effects such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach upset. preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat asthma due to their anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which help to ease breathing and reduce inflammation in the airways. The bioactive constituents of Salvia officinalis, including rosmarinic acid, carnosic acid, and camphor, possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that contribute to its therapeutic effects. By acting as a bronchodilator, Salvia officinalis teas help to relax the muscles in the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of asthma symptoms. The benefits of using Salvia officinalis teas to treat asthma include reduced frequency and severity of asthma attacks, improved lung function, and enhanced overall respiratory health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves or 2 teaspoons of dried leaves.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the leaves in a heat-resistant cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea to make it sweeter.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally 30 minutes before meals, to help manage asthma symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology, Salvia officinalis teas for asthma are not mentioned in the provided study, which actually discusses the effects of Lignosus rhinocerotis mushroom on airways patency and asthma.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be used to treat asthma symptoms, but possible side effects include gastrointestinal upset, such as nausea and diarrhea, as well as allergic reactions like hives or itching. Precautions when using Salvia officinalis teas to treat asthma include avoiding large doses, as they may exacerbate symptoms, and being aware of potential interactions with other medications, such as blood thinners or diabetes medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Ephedra sinica teas, a traditional herbal remedy, have been used for centuries to treat asthma and other respiratory conditions. The properties of this herbal preparation, including its bronchodilatory and anti-inflammatory effects, help to alleviate asthma symptoms by expanding airways and reducing inflammation in the lungs. The bioactive constituents of Ephedra sinica, such as ephedrine and norephedrine, contribute to its therapeutic effects by stimulating the body's natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory responses. The benefits of using Ephedra sinica teas to treat asthma include improved respiratory function, reduced symptoms of wheezing and shortness of breath, and enhanced overall quality of life.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Ephedra sinica leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Ephedra sinica leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or a piece of cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea to sweeten it, if needed.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally in the morning and before bedtime, to help relieve asthma symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Annals of allergy, asthma & immunology : official publication of the American College of Allergy, Asthma, & Immunology, Ephedra sinica teas for asthma may pose a risk of exacerbation of allergies and asthma due to the presence of ephedra.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ephedra sinica teas can be used to treat asthma symptoms, but possible side effects may include rapid heartbeat, high blood pressure, anxiety, insomnia, and stomach upset due to the stimulant properties of ephedrine. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose, monitor blood pressure and heart rate closely, and avoid mixing Ephedra sinica teas with other stimulants, caffeine, or certain medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Astragalus membranaceus teas have been traditionally used to treat asthma due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which help to alleviate symptoms and improve lung function. The bioactive constituents present in Astragalus membranaceus teas, including flavonoids, polysaccharides, and saponins, exhibit bronchodilatory effects, thereby making it easier for patients to breathe. The herbal preparation helps to treat asthma by reducing inflammation, modulating the immune system, and improving lung function, ultimately leading to improved quality of life for patients. The benefits of using Astragalus membranaceus teas to treat asthma include reduced symptoms, improved lung function, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a promising natural remedy for this chronic condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Astragalus membranaceus root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Pour the boiling water over the dried root in a cup or teapot.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the active ingredients to mix with the water.
Strain the mixture into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage asthma symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Zhonghua jie he he hu xi za zhi = Zhonghua jiehe he huxi zazhi = Chinese journal of tuberculosis and respiratory diseases, Astragalus membranaceus teas may be beneficial for asthma patients as it was found to increase FVC values and improve FEV1 and PEF measurements after six weeks of treatment.
preparations.preparation_side_effect:
Astragalus membranaceus teas can be used to potentially alleviate asthma symptoms; however, possible side effects may include interactions with blood thinners, as well as increased risk of bleeding due to its anticoagulant properties and potential lowering of blood pressure.

Precautions include using it in moderation, avoiding excessive consumption, and considering potential interactions with other medications, such as diabetes medications, blood thinners, and certain antidepressants, in order to minimize the risk of adverse effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas have been traditionally used to treat asthma, an inflammatory respiratory disorder. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Curcuma longa, particularly the presence of curcuminoids, help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the airways, thereby alleviating asthma symptoms. The bioactive constituents of Curcuma longa, including curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing airway inflammation and improving lung function. The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas to treat asthma include reduced frequency and severity of asthma attacks, improved lung function, and enhanced overall quality of life.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1/2 teaspoon of dried Curcuma longa root powder.
Heat the water in a pan over medium heat.
Add the Curcuma longa root powder to the water and stir well.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and drink while warm.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Curcuma longa teas for asthma may be effective due to the presence of curcumin, an active phytochemical that can control inflammation and airway remodeling.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be used as a potential treatment for asthma, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high concentration of curcumin, a compound that can irritate the digestive system. To use Curcuma longa teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase it to minimize the risk of side effects, and also to avoid consuming it with other medications or substances that may interact with its active compounds.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula latifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat asthma due to their anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which help to relax the airways and improve breathing. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including linalool and linalyl acetate, contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals and promoting the relaxation of airway smooth muscles. The benefits of using Lavandula latifolia teas to treat asthma include reduced symptoms, improved lung function, and a decrease in the frequency of asthma attacks. Regular consumption of Lavandula latifolia teas may also help to reduce the reliance on conventional medications and improve overall respiratory health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula latifolia flowers.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried flowers and place in a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth.
Heat 1 cup of water in a kettle or on the stove.
Steep the Lavandula latifolia flowers in the hot water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day to help alleviate asthma symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of complementary & integrative medicine, Lavandula latifolia teas are one of the medicinal plants recommended by Persian Medicine for the treatment of asthma, as mentioned in the Canon of Medicine by Avicenna.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula latifolia teas can be used to treat asthma, but possible side effects may include drowsiness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions to take when using this herbal preparation include avoiding consumption if pregnant or breastfeeding, as well as being cautious when driving or operating heavy machinery due to potential drowsiness.

preparation_study: According to African Journal of Traditional, Complementary, and Alternative Medicines : AJTCAM, Eucalyptus globulus teas for asthma were used by traditional healers in the southern region of Togo to treat the disease.
intro_study: According to "The Journal of asthma : official journal of the Association for the Care of Asthma", teas for asthma may be useful in treatment, but there is insufficient evidence to make recommendations for or against their use, and the study quality and safety profile must be balanced for the herb.
